engineGuide.h2: BMW 2 Series Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW 2 Series across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Rear timing chain design caused widespread failures. Defined early F22 replacement demand despite strong torque and economy figures.
compatibleVariants: 218d, 218d M Sport, 220d, 220d M Sport, 220d xDrive, 225d M Sport
commonFailures: Rear timing chain stretch or snap (frequently 60k-80k miles)
DPF differential pressure sensor faults triggering regeneration loops
Inlet manifold flap motor failure causing uneven air distribution
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Front timing drive replaced problematic rear setup. Delivered Euro 6 efficiency and improved long-term reliability for F22/F45.
commonFailures: EGR cooler coolant leaks (pre-2019 production batches)
Turbo actuator sticking due to carbon buildup
AdBlue injector crystallisation blocking supply lines
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 6 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Early 1.6L turbo for entry F22 models. Prone to timing chain and HPFP issues but delivered strong low-end torque for compact segment.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling at startup (50k-70k miles)
High-pressure fuel pump failure triggering hard starting and limp mode
Turbocharger wastegate actuator rod wear causing boost fluctuations
entries.cta: Get quotes for N13B16 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 5 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Compact three-cylinder turbo introduced modular architecture. Focused on urban efficiency and low running costs across F45 Active Tourer.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection design
Water pump and thermostat housing cracks leading to overheating
Turbocharger oil feed line leaks at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for B38B15 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: TwinPower turbo replaced N20/N13. Integrated water-cooled manifold improved thermal management across F22 coupe and F45 variants.
commonFailures: Plastic coolant pipe fractures near the thermostat housing
Timing chain guide wear on early F22/F23 models
Turbo wastegate rattling and actuator rod wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Single twin-scroll turbo inline-six powered early M235i. Delivered smooth power but required diligent cooling system maintenance.
compatibleVariants: M235i, M235i M Sport, M235i xDrive
commonFailures: Electric water pump and thermostat failure at 60k-80k miles
Turbo wastegate actuator rod wear causing boost fluctuation
Oil filter housing gasket leaks leading to coolant contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for N55B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Closed-deck inline-six replaced N55 in M240i. Features twin-scroll turbo and exceptional thermal management for performance variants.
compatibleVariants: M240i, M240i xDrive, M240i M Sport, M240i xDrive Launch Edition
commonFailures: Plastic coolant expansion tank leaks under thermal cycling stress
Oil filter housing gasket degradation around 50k miles
High-pressure fuel injector seal wear causing fuel trim errors
entries.cta: Get quotes for B58B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Motorsport-derived twin-turbo inline-six for M2 Competition. Forged internals and reinforced block handle track-focused driving demands.
commonFailures: Crank hub slippage on early production units (rare but catastrophic)
Turbocharger oil feed line leaks under sustained high-load driving
Clutch and flywear wear accelerated by track use patterns
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: 5 Litre PHEV
entries.history: Petrol-electric plug-in hybrid for F45 Active Tourer. Combined efficiency with electric-only urban capability for commuter flexibility.

entries.code: High-voltage battery coolant pump failure triggering
entries.title: hybrid system faults

commonProblems.h2: BMW 2 Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW 2 Series delivers premium performance, but its high-output B47, N47 and B48/N20 engine families face well-documented failure points that frequently require complete engine replacement. Every rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components engineered specifically to resolve these original design weaknesses, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below as a decision tool to weigh repair viability against full replacement for your specific engine code.

problems.group: BMW 2 Series N47 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW 2 Series N47 Timing Chain Failure - Why Pre-2015 Diesel Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: The original tensioner and guide material in early N47D20 engines degrade under thermal stress, causing chain stretch and catastrophic top-end mechanical interference if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain Kit Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Involves removing cylinder head, fitting upgraded tensioner, reinforced guides and revised chain
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain skip; prevents catastrophic valve/piston damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete stripped block rebuild with upgraded timing components, new oil pump, balanced rotating assembly, bench-tested and ECU-matched
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution when timing failure has caused internal scoring, bent valves or crank damage

problems.recommendation: On a pre-2015 BMW 2 Series N47D20, we strongly advise monitoring cold-start rattling immediately. If caught early, an upgraded preventative timing kit is cost-effective. However, once the chain has stretched past 1.5mm or you hear top-end knocking, internal damage is almost certain and a full rebuilt engine becomes the only reliable long-term option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47D20 engine prices for your BMW 2 Series

problems.group: BMW 2 Series B47 EGR Cooler Cracking
problems.h4: BMW 2 Series B47 EGR Cooler Cracking - Why 2015+ Diesel Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: BMW Service Bulletin SI B12 03 18 confirms the original aluminium EGR cooler housing fractures under repeated thermal cycling, allowing coolant to leak directly into the intake manifold and combustion chambers.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler Replacement & System Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of fractured cooler unit, fitting upgraded cast-iron revision, ECU mapping to disable DTC codes and full cooling circuit flush
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix for minor cracks; MoT emissions compliance must be verified for post-2018 models

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine teardown, hydrostatic crack testing, installation of revised EGR cooler, new injectors, DPF health check and full recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when coolant ingestion has caused hydrolock, bearing washout or severe cylinder scoring

problems.recommendation: A fractured EGR cooler on the B47D20 should never be ignored. While a revised cooler swap can temporarily resolve leaks, prolonged coolant ingress rapidly destroys main bearings and the DPF substrate. If your BMW 2 Series has experienced misfires or white smoke alongside coolant loss, a full rebuilt engine with factory-revision cooling components is the safest and most economical long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get live quotes for rebuilt B47D20 BMW 2 Series engines

problems.group: BMW 2 Series N20 Timing Chain Guide Collapse
problems.h4: BMW 2 Series N20 Timing Chain Guide Collapse - Why Pre-2017 Petrol Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: BMW Critical Service Bulletin CSB 07 2015 highlights premature plastic guide wear in the N20B20 chain tensioning system, leading to guide fragmentation, chain slack, and sudden top-end mechanical failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of timing cover, replacement with reinforced guides, revised tensioner, chain and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents further failure but does not repair existing scoring or bearing damage if noise has progressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine strip, bore honing or liner replacement, forged connecting rod verification, revised timing assembly, dyno validation
repairOptions.longevity: Essential once guide debris has entered the oil gallery or caused bearing spin/crank journal damage

problems.recommendation: The N20B20 timing chain guide failure on the BMW 2 Series is progressive but ultimately catastrophic. If you notice a high-pitched whining or rattling from the timing cover at idle, book an immediate specialist inspection. Once metal shavings contaminate the oil circuit, bearing failure is imminent, and investing in a fully rebuilt N20 engine with upgraded guides is far more reliable than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare N20B20 engine replacement prices for your BMW 2 Series

problems.group: BMW 2 Series B48 Coolant System Leak
problems.h4: BMW 2 Series B48 Coolant System Leak - Why Modern Petrol Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: The original plastic thermostat housing and coolant routing pipes on the B48B20 become brittle under sustained turbo heat, leading to rapid coolant loss, overheating, and potential head gasket compromise or crankcase thermal stress.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Revised Coolant Hose & Thermostat Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of degraded plastic routing pipes, fitting aluminium-reinforced hoses, updated thermostat housing and pressure testing
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative measure if caught before overheating; restores cooling circuit integrity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full block inspection, head resurfacing or replacement, revised cooling system integration, new gaskets, turbo oil line refresh and ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary when prolonged overheating has warped the cylinder head, blown the head gasket, or caused piston scuffing

problems.recommendation: The B48B20 coolant failure on the BMW 2 Series is highly predictable but easily mitigated if addressed early. We recommend replacing the factory plastic coolant routing with BMW’s revised aluminium-spec components as soon as minor seepage appears. However, if your vehicle has entered thermal limp mode or shown coolant/oil mixing, the block or head has likely sustained irreversible damage, making a rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling architecture the only viable option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B48B20 engine prices for your BMW 2 Series

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW 2 Series
finalCta.paragraph: Engine problems on the BMW 2 Series are often highly specific to generation and engine code, but delaying intervention consistently turns manageable faults into total write-offs. Our price comparison platform connects you directly with certified UK BMW 2 Series engine specialists who provide fully rebuilt units with upgraded failure-point components, nationwide delivery, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Stop comparing main dealer premiums-get transparent, specialist-backed quotes for your exact chassis and engine code, and return to the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW 2 Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW 2 Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW 2 Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the BMW 2 Series is detailed below, complete with compatible engine codes, generation years, and verified failure notes to ensure precise replacement matching.

items.title: BMW 2 Series 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced primarily for the F45 Active Tourer and F46 Gran Tourer platforms, the compact 1.5L triple-cylinder diesel was engineered to deliver strong low-end torque while maintaining exceptional urban fuel economy and lower company car taxation. Featuring BMW’s EfficientDynamics lightweight architecture and a high-pressure common-rail injection system, it serves as the entry-level diesel option with refined NVH characteristics for daily commuting.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve carbon buildup under frequent short-trip driving cycles
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 2 Series 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: BMW 2 Series 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Serving as the mechanical backbone of the BMW 2 Series diesel lineup across all F and G chassis generations, the 2.0L inline-four balances robust towing capability with long-distance refinement and Euro 6 emissions compliance. Early F22/F23 variants utilised the N47 architecture before transitioning to the thermally optimised B47 block, which features a revised cylinder head, upgraded timing chain assembly, and sequential turbocharging in the twin-turbo 225d application.
commonFailurePoints: B47 EGR cooler cracking under sustained thermal load
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 2 Series 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW 2 Series 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Deployed across the F22 Coupe and transverse F45/F46 MPV variants, the entry-level 1.5L three-cylinder petrol replaces older naturally aspirated units with advanced twin-scroll turbocharging and Valvetronic variable valve lift. This displacement prioritises smooth power delivery and reduced internal friction, offering a practical, low-tax alternative for urban and suburban driving while maintaining BMW’s characteristic throttle response.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare BMW 2 Series 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 2 Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Dominating the mainstream petrol range, the 2.0L four-cylinder evolved from the direct-injection N20 to the thermally resilient B48 architecture, which integrates an exhaust manifold into the cylinder head and utilises a reinforced closed-deck block for improved durability. This unit powers the majority of 220i and 228i specifications across rear-wheel and xDrive configurations, delivering a proven balance of mid-range torque and high-RPM flexibility for both manual and automatic transmissions.
commonFailurePoints: B48 plastic coolant routing pipe fractures under turbo heat
items.cta: Compare BMW 2 Series 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 2 Series 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Reserved exclusively for M Performance flagship variants, the 3.0L turbocharged inline-six represents the engineering pinnacle of the 2 Series petrol range, featuring precision-balanced crankshafts, magnesium-aluminium block construction, and advanced direct fuel injection. Initially deployed with single-turbo N55 architecture in the M235i, the platform transitioned to the B58’s twin-scroll turbo design and reinforced bottom-end components to sustain higher boost pressures and track-level thermal loads.
commonFailurePoints: B58 thermostat housing/coolant seal degradation over time
items.cta: Compare BMW 2 Series 3.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW 2 Series Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW 2 Series 2.0L MHEV Engines
items.description: Introduced with the G42 Coupe and U06 Active Tourer generations, the 48V mild-hybrid system pairs a belt-starter generator with the established B48 and B58 powertrains to enable torque fill, extended coasting, and smoother start-stop operation. While sharing the core displacement architecture with standard petrol variants, MHEV models feature reinforced high-voltage wiring, updated ECU mapping, and optimised regenerative braking integration to improve real-world efficiency without altering fundamental mechanical reliability.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW 2 Series 2.0L hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: BMW 2 Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major engine change across the BMW 2 Series production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their model year engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: BMW's inaugural 2 Series model year introduced rear-wheel-drive coupe/convertible and transverse MPV variants, establishing the N47 diesel and N20/N55 petrol line-ups.
keyChanges: F22 Coupe and F23 Convertible platforms debuted alongside F45/F46 Active/Gran Tourer
N47D20 diesel replaced older M47 architecture with improved emissions compliance
N55B30 turbocharged inline-six launched as new M235i flagship
Early N20B20 petrol carried over from previous generation 1 Series
knownFor: Strong initial UK sales volume driving high long-term replacement demand
Early N47 units retain rear-mounted timing chain tensioner vulnerability
N55 turbo delivers renowned M Performance character and robust bottom-end
N20 early batches carry CSB 07 2015 timing guide collapse risk

faq.h2: BMW 2 Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW 2 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine typically costs £1,650-£4,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Main dealer replacements often exceed £5,000. Reconditioned units with 12-month warranties offer the best value for most owners seeking reliable, MoT-compliant replacements from certified UK specialists.
keyPoints: B47D20 diesel rebuilds average £1,950-£3,600 supply-only across UK specialists
N47D20 legacy units cost less but require mandatory timing chain upgrades
Petrol B48B20 replacements range £1,800-£3,800 depending on output specification
Supply & fit adds £650-£950 labour at certified BMW independent garages
All prices exclude VAT, delivery and ECU adaptation fees where applicable
items.cta: Compare live BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is the N47 diesel engine in the BMW 2 Series reliable?
items.answer: The N47D20 diesel in pre-2015 BMW 2 Series models suffers from well-documented timing chain tensioner failures, often causing catastrophic engine damage between 60k-100k miles. Post-2015 B47 replacements resolved most issues, making later diesel variants significantly more dependable for high-mileage ownership and reducing urgent replacement enquiries.
keyPoints: N47 timing chains stretch due to inadequate tensioner design per BMW CSB 03 2014
Cold-start rattling indicates imminent chain failure requiring immediate specialist inspection
Upgraded tensioner kits can prevent failure if fitted before catastrophic damage occurs
B47D20 engines from 2015 onwards feature revised chain geometry and improved reliability
Rebuilt N47 units with upgraded components offer cost-effective replacement versus dealer pricing
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt N47D20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: What causes the B47 EGR cooler to fail on BMW 2 Series diesels?
items.answer: BMW 2 Series B47D20 engines suffer EGR cooler cracking due to thermal stress on aluminium housings, documented in service bulletin SI B12 03 18. Coolant ingress into the intake can cause hydrolock, bearing washout or DPF contamination, often requiring full engine replacement rather than simple cooler swaps.
keyPoints: Aluminium EGR housings fracture under repeated heating cycles common in urban driving
Coolant leakage triggers white smoke, misfires and potential hydrolock damage to pistons
Revised cast-iron EGR coolers are mandatory in all rebuilt B47D20 replacement engines
DPF contamination from coolant ingress often requires expensive substrate replacement or cleaning
Early detection via coolant loss monitoring can prevent catastrophic internal engine damage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt B47D20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded EGR coolers included

items.question: Should I buy a used BMW 2 Series with the N20 petrol engine?
items.answer: The N20B20 petrol engine in 2014-2017 BMW 2 Series models carries a known timing chain guide collapse risk per BMW CSB 07 2015. While affordable to purchase, budget £1,800-£3,200 for a rebuilt N20 with upgraded guides or consider post-2017 B48 variants for greater long-term reliability.
keyPoints: Plastic timing chain guides fragment, causing chain slack and top-end mechanical interference
High-pitched whining at idle signals impending guide failure requiring immediate specialist assessment
Rebuilt N20 engines include reinforced guides, revised tensioners and dyno validation before dispatch
B48B20 engines from 2017 onwards resolved guide issues with improved thermal management
Pre-purchase compression testing and service history review are essential for N20-equipped examples
items.cta: Find rebuilt N20B20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded timing components and warranty

items.question: How long do BMW 2 Series engines typically last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained BMW 2 Series engines can exceed 150,000 miles, but failure patterns vary significantly by engine code. B47/B48 units often reach 120k-180k miles with regular servicing, while N47/N20 engines frequently require replacement between 60k-100k miles due to inherent design weaknesses unrelated to owner maintenance quality.
keyPoints: Full service history with BMW-approved oils extends B47/B48 engine life significantly
N47/N20 timing component failures occur regardless of oil change frequency or driving style
Urban short-trip usage accelerates DPF and EGR issues on diesel variants across all codes
Performance variants like M240i B58 engines benefit from closed-deck blocks and robust internals
Rebuilt engines with upgraded failure-point components often outlast original factory specifications
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded components for extended reliability

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series engine or buying another car?
items.answer: Replacing a BMW 2 Series engine costs £1,650-£4,500 versus £8,000-£15,000 for a comparable used replacement vehicle. If the chassis, transmission and interior remain sound, a rebuilt engine with 12-month warranty offers superior value and retains your known service history versus an unknown used car.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded failure-point components addressing original design weaknesses
Supply & fit packages from certified specialists reduce installation risk and calibration errors
Retaining your existing 2 Series avoids depreciation hit and new vehicle finance costs
MoT emissions compliance is guaranteed with rebuilt engines versus uncertain used car history
Specialist rebuilders offer 12-month unlimited mileage warranties exceeding most used car dealer coverage
items.cta: Get tailored BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes to compare against used car prices

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used BMW 2 Series engines?
items.answer: Used BMW 2 Series engines are untested donor units (£1,250-£2,800). Reconditioned units replace worn seals and bearings (£1,850-£4,200). Rebuilt engines undergo full teardown with upgraded failure-point components (£2,100-£4,500). All carry 12-month warranties, but rebuilt units offer the highest reliability for known failure patterns.
keyPoints: Used engines require compression testing and carry higher uncertainty regarding internal wear
Reconditioned units restore factory tolerances but may not address original design weaknesses
Rebuilt engines include mandatory upgrades for N47 timing, B47 EGR and N20 guide issues
Remanufactured options offer factory-spec precision but at premium pricing versus specialist rebuilds
All EngineMarket suppliers provide minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty regardless of condition type
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement BMW 2 Series engine pass MoT and ULEZ requirements?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines supplied through EngineMarket are calibrated for MoT emissions compliance and ULEZ eligibility. DPF health validation, ECU adaptation and exhaust system integrity checks ensure your replacement unit meets current London, Birmingham and other clean air zone standards upon installation and subsequent testing.
keyPoints: Diesel replacements include DPF substrate validation and regeneration capability verification before dispatch
Petrol B48/B58 units feature revised coolant routing to prevent overheating-related emissions failures
ECU flashing ensures diagnostic codes clear and readiness monitors complete for MoT testing
ULEZ compliance depends on original vehicle registration date, not engine replacement status
Specialist installers provide post-fit emissions testing documentation to support MoT certification
items.cta: Find MoT-compliant rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines with ULEZ validation documentation included

items.question: Can I fit a B48 engine from a 3 Series into my BMW 2 Series?
items.answer: Cross-chassis BMW 2 Series engine swaps require specialist ECU coding, wiring harness adaptation and mounting point verification. While B48B20 blocks are physically similar across F22/F30 platforms, chassis-specific calibrations for xDrive, transmission and ancillaries mean direct swaps rarely work without professional adaptation and risk voiding warranties.
keyPoints: F22 Coupe and F30 3 Series share B48 architecture but differ in ECU mapping tables
xDrive transfer case calibration requires chassis-specific software adaptation during engine installation
Ancillary bracket positioning and coolant routing vary between 2 Series and 3 Series applications
Specialist rebuilders can pre-adapt replacement engines to your exact chassis code and VIN
Attempting DIY swaps without BMW ISTA coding tools risks immobiliser lockout and sensor errors
items.cta: Enquire about chassis-specific BMW 2 Series engine replacements with pre-adapted ECU coding

items.question: How do I know if my BMW 2 Series needs a full engine replacement?
items.answer: Key indicators include persistent knocking, white/blue exhaust smoke, coolant-oil mixing, or loss of compression across multiple cylinders. If diagnostics confirm internal scoring, bearing failure or hydrolock damage, repair costs often exceed replacement value, making a rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine the most economical long-term solution.
keyPoints: Cold-start rattling on N47/N20 engines signals timing component failure requiring immediate assessment
Coolant in oil or white smoke indicates head gasket or EGR cooler failure on B47/B48
Compression testing below 120 PSI across cylinders confirms internal damage needing full rebuild
Metal shavings in oil or sump indicate bearing wear requiring block teardown and machining
Specialist diagnostic assessment before committing to repair versus replacement avoids wasted expenditure
items.cta: Get a specialist diagnostic assessment for your BMW 2 Series engine before deciding
